title: Frameworks
subtitle: Every concept from the Product Lifecycle, as a searchable index.
intro: This is the flat view. Concepts across seven lifecycle stages, alphabetically sorted. Use the search box to find a concept by name, or filter by stage to narrow the list. If you want the narrative view - stages, flow, journey - see the Product Lifecycle page.
footnote: The framework pages above are being written one at a time. Each is a short reference page on how I think about that concept - not a textbook definition. New pages are added regularly.

concepts.title: Accessibility
concepts.blurb: Building products that work for users with disabilities through inclusive design and standards.

concepts.title: Agile, Scrum & Kanban
concepts.blurb: Iterative delivery frameworks that structure how teams plan, ship, and adapt.

concepts.title: Assumption Mapping & Risk Reduction
concepts.blurb: Surfacing the unverified beliefs underneath a plan and testing the riskiest ones first.

concepts.title: Beta Testing & Early Feedback Loops
concepts.blurb: Releasing to a controlled group before general launch to catch real-world issues early.

concepts.title: Bug Triage & Severity Prioritisation
concepts.blurb: Deciding which bugs block launch, which can wait, and how to communicate the call.

concepts.title: Capturing Learnings & Postmortem Insights
concepts.blurb: Extracting durable lessons from a product or feature that didn't survive.

concepts.title: Collecting & Synthesising User Feedback
concepts.blurb: Turning scattered user input into patterns that drive product decisions.

concepts.title: Communicating a Product Sunset to Users
concepts.blurb: Telling users a product is being retired in a way that preserves trust.

concepts.title: Continuous Improvement Roadmapping
concepts.blurb: Building an evolving post-launch roadmap that responds to real usage data.

concepts.title: Coordinating a Cross-Functional Launch
concepts.blurb: Orchestrating product, engineering, design, marketing, sales, and support to land a launch.

concepts.title: Creating a Pre-Launch Plan
concepts.blurb: Defining the milestones, dependencies, and readiness checks that precede launch.

concepts.title: Customer Communication & Feature Announcement
concepts.blurb: Telling users about a new feature in a way that drives adoption.

concepts.title: Customer Persona Creation
concepts.blurb: Building a realistic snapshot of a user segment to focus product decisions.

concepts.title: Data Migration & Transition Planning
concepts.blurb: Moving user data and workflows safely when retiring a product or feature.

concepts.title: Defining Success Metrics
concepts.blurb: Choosing the metrics that will tell you whether the product is working.

concepts.title: Design Handoff
concepts.blurb: Transferring design assets to engineering in a way that preserves intent.

concepts.title: Engineer & Designer Collaboration
concepts.blurb: Working with engineering and design as partners, not as resources.

concepts.title: Evaluating Pivot vs Sunset vs Maintain
concepts.blurb: Deciding whether a struggling product should change direction, be killed, or be maintained.

concepts.title: Feature Flagging & Gradual Rollouts
concepts.blurb: Releasing new features to controlled cohorts before exposing them to all users.

concepts.title: Feature Prioritisation Techniques
concepts.blurb: Choosing what to build next from a longer list of candidates.

concepts.title: First-Response Metrics & Live Monitoring
concepts.blurb: Watching the metrics that tell you whether a launch is going well in real time.

concepts.title: Hypothesis Creation & Validation
concepts.blurb: Turning product instincts into testable hypotheses and validating them with users.

concepts.title: Identifying & Addressing Drop-Offs
concepts.blurb: Finding where users abandon a flow and diagnosing why.

concepts.title: Identifying When to Sunset a Product or Feature
concepts.blurb: Detecting the signals - usage, cost, strategy - that justify deprecation.

concepts.title: Instrumentation
concepts.blurb: Setting up the analytics that will let you measure what matters after launch.

concepts.title: Internal Enablement: Sales, Support, and Ops
concepts.blurb: Preparing internal teams to support and sell a new product.

concepts.title: Jobs To Be Done
concepts.blurb: Understanding why users hire a product - and why they fire it.

concepts.title: Managing Technical Debt
concepts.blurb: Balancing the velocity of shipping against the long-term cost of shortcuts.

concepts.title: Market & Competitor Analysis
concepts.blurb: Mapping the landscape a product will compete in before committing to a direction.

concepts.title: Measuring Adoption & Retention
concepts.blurb: Tracking whether users actually use the product over time.

concepts.title: MVP Definition & Scope Setting
concepts.blurb: Defining the smallest version of a product that delivers real value and tests a real hypothesis.

concepts.title: North Star Metric Review & Long-Term Success
concepts.blurb: Revisiting whether the metric you chose still captures what matters.

concepts.title: Onboarding Experience for New Features
concepts.blurb: Introducing a new feature to existing users without disrupting their workflow.

concepts.title: Opportunity Sizing & Market Potential
concepts.blurb: Estimating how big the addressable opportunity is before committing resources.

concepts.title: Opportunity Solution Tree
concepts.blurb: Mapping the opportunity space before you pick a solution; testing assumptions before you commit.

concepts.title: Post-Launch Experimentation & A/B Testing
concepts.blurb: Running structured experiments to learn what improves the product.

concepts.title: Pre-Launch Go/No-Go Decision Framework
concepts.blurb: Structuring the call on whether the product is ready to ship.

concepts.title: Problem Discovery vs Solution Obsession
concepts.blurb: Falling in love with the problem before falling in love with the solution.

concepts.title: Problem Statement Template
concepts.blurb: A short, shared, solution-free definition of what you're solving - and how you'll know it's fixed.

concepts.title: Product Roadmapping
concepts.blurb: Mapping what gets built when, and what gets traded off in service of it.

concepts.title: Product Vision Alignment
concepts.blurb: Connecting product decisions to a coherent longer-term vision.

concepts.title: QA Planning
concepts.blurb: Structuring quality assurance so it catches issues before they reach users.

concepts.title: Regression Testing & Rollback Plans
concepts.blurb: Verifying that new releases don't break existing functionality, with rollback paths if they do.

concepts.title: Scope Creep Handling
concepts.blurb: Holding the line on what's in and what's out as a project unfolds.

concepts.title: Setting Product Goals
concepts.blurb: Defining the outcomes a product is trying to achieve in a given time period.

concepts.title: Stakeholder Alignment & Buy-In
concepts.blurb: Getting the people who matter on side before execution begins.

concepts.title: Tracking & Unblocking Progress
concepts.blurb: Spotting where work is stuck and clearing the path forward.

concepts.title: Usability Testing for PMs
concepts.blurb: Watching real users attempt to use a product to find where it breaks down.

concepts.title: User Acceptance Testing
concepts.blurb: Verifying with real users that the product meets the original requirements.

concepts.title: User Interviews & Research
concepts.blurb: Talking to real users to understand needs that data alone can't reveal.

concepts.title: User Stories & Acceptance Criteria
concepts.blurb: Writing requirements that engineering and design can act on without ambiguity.

concepts.title: Value Proposition Design
concepts.blurb: Articulating why a user would choose this product over the alternatives.

concepts.title: Writing a Great PRD
concepts.blurb: Producing a product requirements document that aligns teams and reduces rework.

concepts.title: Writing Good OKRs
concepts.blurb: Setting objectives and key results that focus a team on outcomes that matter.
